mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, forcing markets to price in aggressive future central bank tightening and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns when real yields remain positive, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 across tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals economic stagnation and deteriorating corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below the threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total payrolls confirm synchronized supply-side expansion and labor absorption, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when inventory accumulation exceeds sales growth, or interaction effects vanish in walk-forward validation.
